commands.review_summary:
The PR carries Telegram Bot API error codes across the isolated ingress worker boundary, restarts isolated polling on 409 conflicts with webhook cleanup and transport rotation, and exposes polling conflicts and stalls through channel status.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +103. Total +151 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main’s getUpdates-only worker drops the numeric Bot API code, and the parent rethrows a 409 because it is not classified as a recoverable network error, which gives a high-confidence source reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should review the protected-label PR and require the missing real Telegram behavior proof before merge; no narrow code repair is currently indicated.

target.title: fix(telegram): survive getUpdates conflicts in isolated polling ingress
target.body:
## Summary

A getUpdates `409 Conflict` (second poller on the same bot token, or a stale webhook) kills the whole Telegram account in isolated ingress mode — the default. The ingress worker flattens Bot API errors into a message string, so the parent session cannot tell a conflict apart from a fatal `401` and rethrows out of `runUntilAbort` (`extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts`). The core supervisor then burns one of `MAX_RESTART_ATTEMPTS = 10` (preserved across auto-restarts, `src/gateway/server-channels.ts`) and eventually gives up permanently — while the classic polling cycle has always handled the same 409 in-session with webhook re-clear, transport rotation (#69787), and a duplicate-poller hint.

Changes:
- The ingress worker preserves the Bot API `error_code` on getUpdates failures and posts it in `poll-error` messages (typed worker contract).
- The isolated cycle treats 409 like the classic path: re-clear webhook, rotate transport, restart with backoff + the duplicate-poller hint, instead of crashing the account. Fatal errors (e.g. 401) still propagate.
- The conflict hint and stall detection now reach channel status (`lastError`/`connected`), not just the gateway log. Previously a classic-path conflict loop showed `connected: true` with no error for up to 30 minutes, and the actionable diagnosis never reached `openclaw channels status`.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` — 54 passed.
- Regression proof (prod files swapped to `origin/main`, new tests kept): `restarts isolated ingress on a getUpdates conflict instead of crashing the account` fails with the account-crash (`runUntilAbort` rejects with `Conflict: terminated by other getUpdates request`); the status assertions in the classic conflict and stall tests fail with no patch published. All pass with this branch's code.
- Testbox `check:changed` green (`tbx_01ktsa6ds3121d18zk2mq7470k`): typecheck extensions + extension tests, oxlint, import cycles, guards.
- Not tested: live two-poller 409 against the real Bot API; the worker runtime file itself has no direct test (boots as a worker thread; covered via the typed message contract and session-level tests).
target.author: obviyus
